hyperedges.label: RLS Access Control Flow on manifestations
Reverie Default Security Header Set (per response)

nodes.label: Enforcement Guidelines Ladder
nodes.rationale: Adopts Contributor Covenant v2.0 ladder rather than v3.0 because v2.0 tiers map cleanly onto GitHub's per-user moderation tools; v3.0's Temporarily Limited Activities tier requires per-user activity limits GitHub does not natively support.

nodes.label: Conduct Report Channel (Private Advisory)
nodes.rationale: Reuses the project's private security disclosure channel; conduct reports handled with the same confidentiality as security reports.

nodes.label: Reverie (project)
nodes.rationale: Self-hosted ebook library manager; pre-alpha; AGPL-3.0; threat model is a multi-user exposed instance.

nodes.label: Reverie Security Posture
nodes.rationale: Backend owns all security response headers; reverse proxies must pass them through unchanged. Target A+ on securityheaders.com and Mozilla Observatory.

nodes.label: Contributor License Agreement
nodes.rationale: PR submission assigns copyright to maintainer (John Unkovich) to preserve dual-license option while keeping AGPL-3.0 for the community.

nodes.label: Mandatory TDD Policy
nodes.rationale: No feature/bug fix complete without happy-path, negative, and edge-case tests; PRs without tests are not approved.

nodes.label: Renovate Dependency Bot
nodes.rationale: Weekly schedule; security updates bypass schedule. Don't file separate dependency-bump PRs unless patching an unflagged advisory.

nodes.label: Reverie Threat Model (multi-user exposed instance)
nodes.rationale: Defaults lean toward the exposed-internet case; single-user LAN deployments still benefit but aren't the primary target.

nodes.label: Vulnerability Reporting (GitHub Private Advisory)
nodes.rationale: 72-hour ack, 7-day initial assessment, 90-day fix target; public disclosure before patch gives operators no time to react.

nodes.label: Supported Versions Policy
nodes.rationale: Pre-v1.0; only the most recent 0.x release receives security patches.

nodes.label: FRBR-Inspired Data Model (Works/Manifestations)
nodes.rationale: Works represent abstract titles; Manifestations represent concrete files. Separation lets multiple editions, formats, translations share metadata.

nodes.label: series table (self-referential)
nodes.rationale: ON DELETE SET NULL on parent_id orphans children rather than cascade-delete entire series trees.

nodes.label: Row Level Security on manifestations
nodes.rationale: Six per-operation policies; children cannot UPDATE/DELETE manifestations (shared library records); they manage visibility through shelf_items.

nodes.label: Session Variable Contract (app.current_user_id)
nodes.rationale: SET LOCAL is transaction-scoped, auto-resets on commit/rollback, safe with connection pools. Unset variable returns NULL → all visibility checks fail closed.

nodes.label: chk_child_role_sync CHECK constraint
nodes.rationale: Ensures is_child=true only when role='child'; role controls permissions, is_child drives content filtering — they must stay consistent.

nodes.label: series_works.position NUMERIC
nodes.rationale: Allows fractional ordering (e.g., 1.5 for novellas between volumes 1 and 2).

nodes.label: crosscheck custom skill
nodes.rationale: Dual-model adversarial review (Claude Opus subagent + Gemini 3.1 Pro CLI). Default for pre-merge code review; santa-method is Claude-only fallback.

nodes.label: install.sh master script
nodes.rationale: Idempotent; runs every workspace start. Wraps each major block in set +e subshells so one failure doesn't block the rest.

nodes.label: dotfiles repository (junkovich/dotfiles)
nodes.rationale: Source of truth — direct $HOME edits get clobbered on workspace rebuild.

nodes.label: Content Security Policy and Security Headers (operator doc)
nodes.norm_label: content security policy and security headers (operator doc)

nodes.label: Hash-Based HTML CSP (sha256 inline FOUC)
nodes.rationale: Permits one known inline FOUC script via sha256-... hash; no unsafe-inline for scripts.

nodes.label: Locked-Down API CSP (default-src 'none')
nodes.rationale: APIs never render, so default-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'none'; base-uri 'none'.

nodes.label: HSTS Opt-In (REVERIE_BEHIND_HTTPS)
nodes.rationale: Disabled by default because container speaks plain HTTP. includeSubDomains is a footgun on shared apexes; preload requires both subdomains and HTTPS flags.

nodes.label: CSP Violation Reporting (REVERIE_CSP_REPORT_ENDPOINT)
nodes.rationale: Emitted as both report-uri (legacy) and Reporting-Endpoints/report-to (modern Reporting API). URL flows into a header so rejects ", ;, CR, LF to prevent header-splitting injection.

nodes.label: Dev vs Prod CSP Differences
nodes.rationale: Dev relaxations (unsafe-inline, unsafe-eval, HMR WS) live in vite.config.ts and only apply to npm run dev — never ship to prod.

nodes.label: Self-Hosted woff2 Fonts (font-src 'self')
nodes.rationale: Code-declared font policy in csp.rs::build_html_csp; no runtime knob so every deployment carries an identical, auditable policy.

nodes.label: reverie_theme cookie (non-HttpOnly, Secure)
nodes.rationale: Intentionally not HttpOnly because JavaScript must read it synchronously before React hydrates to avoid theme flicker. Carries no PII (literal string system/light/dark). Secure always set; localhost is a browser-recognised secure context so dev still works.

nodes.label: style-src 'unsafe-inline' Pragmatic Concession
nodes.rationale: Concession for Tailwind JIT inline style attrs and Radix UI runtime portal positioning. CSS injection impact is far narrower than script injection; can be dropped by editing csp.rs::build_html_csp once Tailwind/Radix runtime inline styles are eliminated.

nodes.label: Target A+ on securityheaders.com / Mozilla Observatory
nodes.norm_label: target a+ on securityheaders.com / mozilla observatory

nodes.label: Reverse Proxy Topology (operator doc)
nodes.rationale: Single container serves HTML frontend and API from one port (default 3000). No separate frontend container.

nodes.label: nginx Reverse Proxy Sample
nodes.rationale: Do NOT add_header X-Frame-Options or CSP at the proxy — stacking them confuses browsers and breaks per-route CSP differentiation.

nodes.label: Direct Exposure (dev/LAN)
nodes.rationale: Browsers see uniform headers and route-appropriate CSP, no HSTS. Do not expose direct-HTTP deployment to the internet.

nodes.label: Boutique Cinematic Design Register
nodes.rationale: Warm canvases, type-led hierarchy, single decisive accent (Reverie Gold). Reverie is a self-hosted ebook library for people who want their library to look like a library.

nodes.label: Brand Identity Source of Truth (reverie-branding repo)
nodes.rationale: unkos-dev/reverie-branding is the canonical spec; docs site embeds load-bearing parts inline; any drift resolved in branding's favour.
nodes.norm_label: brand identity source of truth (reverie-branding repo)

nodes.label: State Without Hue Invariant
nodes.rationale: State communicates through typography weight, surface opacity, motion, and gold accent — never a state-coded hue. Token set deliberately omits --color-success/warning/danger/info/neutral. Adding any hue-coded state token requires a separate brand-aligned decision.

nodes.label: Reverie Gold Accent (#C9A961 dark / #8E6F38 light)
nodes.rationale: Signature affordance for primary actions, focus rings, recovery actions only. NOT a hover treatment. shadcn primitives shipping with bg-accent for hover/focus rebind to --color-hover instead.

nodes.label: Warm Neutrals (Ink/Cream/Parchment)
nodes.rationale: Ink #0E0D0A, Cream #E8E0D0, Parchment #E8DCC2. No pure black, no pure white.

nodes.label: Motion as Co-Equal Axis
nodes.rationale: 200–300ms interaction feedback budget; ≤300ms page transitions. Reduced-motion preferences disable loading pulse, status-dot pulse, hover lift — they're feedback affordances, not content.

nodes.label: Three Theme Preferences (system/light/dark)
nodes.rationale: One cookie, one FOUC script. reverie_theme survives logout by design — device state, not session state.

nodes.label: Light-Theme Accent axe Deviation
nodes.rationale: #8E6F38 passes UI-component 3:1 and large-text contrast against Parchment but fails normal-text 4.5:1. axe-core flags violations on Light surfaces with bg-accent on normal body text — brand mitigation is restriction to focus rings, large CTAs, recovery actions.

nodes.label: --color-fg-faint Decorative-Only Rule
nodes.rationale: opacity-50 × text-fg-faint falls below AA in both themes; that's why disabled-state mapping uses text-fg-muted instead.

nodes.label: What We Don't Build (no framework, no token-name framework, no runtime theme editor)
nodes.rationale: Design system is intentionally narrow — serves Reverie, not arbitrary downstream consumers. Token names map 1:1 to brand identity palette. Operators can theme through CSS variables at deployment time.
nodes.norm_label: what we don't build (no framework, no token-name framework, no runtime theme editor)

nodes.label: Slot Mark
nodes.rationale: Single rectangular slot; the negative space of a bookshelf.

nodes.label: Lockup Component (frontend/src/components/Lockup.tsx)
nodes.rationale: Canonical render of Slot + wordmark in horizontal lockup; documented exemption to hex-literal ban — must render correctly even before themes/index.css resolves (e.g. on the OIDC error page).

nodes.label: --color-hover Decoupling from Gold
nodes.rationale: Decouples shadcn primitives' hover/focus from gold register; dropdown/select items light up at --color-hover (= --color-surface-2) instead of saturating gold, so brand --color-accent stays the unambiguous signature for primary actions, focus rings, recovery actions.

nodes.label: 4px Spacing Scale
nodes.rationale: Tailwind default spacing scale is the runtime; named tokens not introduced because the scale is conventional and rename cost is high.

nodes.label: loading-pulse Keyframe (no --color-loading)
nodes.rationale: opacity 1 ↔ 0.85, ~1.6s — region pulses opacity rather than introducing a state-coded color token.

nodes.label: Theme Architecture (cookie + html data-theme + DB row)
nodes.rationale: Three places store state — reverie_theme cookie (canonical preference), html[data-theme] (resolved effective theme, never 'system'), users.theme_preference (per-user, follows the user across devices).
nodes.norm_label: theme architecture (cookie + html data-theme + db row)

nodes.label: Cookie Name Three-Place Rule (UNK-105)
nodes.rationale: String 'reverie_theme' lives in backend/src/auth/theme_cookie.rs (THEME_COOKIE_NAME), frontend/src/fouc/fouc.js, frontend/src/lib/theme/cookie.ts. All three MUST change together; cross-stack drift tracked under UNK-105.

nodes.label: Cookie Attribute Parity Contract (set_theme_cookie ↔ writeThemeCookie)
nodes.rationale: Drift between sides produces two cookies of the same name with divergent attributes; FOUC's document.cookie.split('; ') then matches non-deterministically.

nodes.label: FOUC Mechanism (vite-plugins/csp-hash.ts → dist/csp-hashes.json → backend dist_validation.rs)
nodes.rationale: Blocking inline <script> injected at <!-- reverie:fouc-hash --> marker; body in fouc.js (plain ES5, self-invoking, try/catch fallback to light). vite build emits dist/csp-hashes.json containing SHA-256 read by backend at startup. CSP hash-based — no nonce, no backend templating.

nodes.label: Theme Cookie Lifecycle (survives logout)
nodes.rationale: Device state, not session state. Matches industry precedent (GitHub color_mode, MDN site preference, Audiobookshelf, Jellyfin, Kavita) and shared-device rationale: a device's user-distinct theme survives session sign-out without leaking identity.

nodes.label: Future Session-State Cookie Contrast Rule
nodes.rationale: Any future session-state cookie MUST be HttpOnly and MUST clear on logout. reverie_theme is the explicit counterexample; contrast documented at backend/src/auth/theme_cookie.rs module header and cross-referenced from operator-facing CSP doc.

nodes.label: Design System Testing Scope
nodes.rationale: Step 10 design system tested at two distinct bars; deliberate split — snapshotting visual output in per-component unit tests would lock styling into a pixel-brittle contract that Step 11 cannot move. Per-component visual regression tooling not adopted; acceptance bar is axe-core hard gate plus human review.

nodes.label: Custom ESLint Hex-Literal Rule
nodes.rationale: Raw-hex outside token files is a lint error — enforces token-only use of brand palette.

nodes.label: Route-Gating Production-Build Assertion
nodes.rationale: /design/* explore tree is excluded from production bundles.

nodes.label: <!-- reverie:fouc-hash --> Marker
nodes.rationale: Replaced at build by csp-hash Vite plugin with the inline FOUC script body and matching SHA-256.

nodes.label: Brand Asset References (favicon, OG card, apple-touch-icon)
nodes.norm_label: brand asset references (favicon, og card, apple-touch-icon)

nodes.label: Fontshare Self-Hosted Variable woff2
nodes.rationale: CDN delivery blocked because api.fontshare.com/v2/css sets _fontshare_key cookie — Chromium ORB treats as credentialed and blocks. Self-hosting is the only viable delivery path; also strengthens CSP from font-src 'self' https://cdn.fontshare.com to font-src 'self', and italic axes are not exposed by the public weight CSS API.

nodes.label: No @fontsource Package for Author/Satoshi
nodes.rationale: @fontsource indexes only the Google catalogue; Author and Satoshi ship from Fontshare. JetBrains Mono self-hosted from JetBrains GitHub release for delivery-mechanism consistency.

nodes.label: FFL Clause-02 Acceptance
nodes.rationale: Fontshare Free EULA prohibits public-server uploads and non-Fontshare font serving — committing into open-source repo is a formal violation. Accepted because: ORB on CDN blocks the cookie-bearing CSS API; self-hosted CSP is materially stronger; fallback is paid commercial license + on-prem mirror with mechanical URL substitution; risk surfaces to a single party (ITF) with a single resolution path.

nodes.label: SHA256SUMS Integrity Verification
nodes.rationale: CI workflow .github/workflows/ci.yml runs sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS as 'Verify font integrity' step.

nodes.label: JetBrains Mono OFL-1.1
nodes.rationale: Permissive — no FFL constraint.

hyperedges.label: RLS Access Control Flow on manifestations
Reverie Default Security Header Set (per response)
